description_long: This object consists of two hand drums, each with a circular frame and a taut membrane stretched across the top. The left drum features a dark, almost black drumhead and a red-and-black patterned shell, while the right drum has a light beige drumhead and a white shell. Both drums are fitted with metallic jingles set into slots around their sides, typical of tambourines. The shells appear to be made of wood or a wood-like material, with the metallic jingles providing a contrasting accent. The drums are compact and shallow, designed to be played by hand, and their surfaces are smooth and well-finished. These instruments are commonly used in street performances, parades, or community gatherings in urban environments.
description: A pair of hand drums with circular frames, one with a red-and-black shell and dark head, the other with a white shell and beige head, both featuring metallic jingles around the sides.
description_view_0: Both drums are visible side by side, showing the contrasting shell colors and the arrangement of metallic jingles.
description_view_1: The white drum is in the foreground, highlighting its beige drumhead and gold jingles, while the red-and-black drum is partially obscured behind.
description_view_2: The drums are reversed, with the red-and-black drum in the foreground, emphasizing its dark head and patterned shell.
description_view_3: The red-and-black drum is in front, showing its dark drumhead and the circular cutouts for the jingles, with the white drum behind.
